final_prompt.prompt:
Create a museum-grade infographic plate for Chinese ethnic minority costume, formatted as a National Museum exhibition panel.

Style/medium: needs a "museum specimen plate" aesthetic — references in the spirit of National Museum of China exhibition graphics, Victorian natural-history illustration plates, 故宫博物院文物图录 print tradition; exact stylistic execution is yours
Composition: must follow fixed five-zone layout: top header band, left structural breakdown zone with leader lines, upper-right material/texture zone, middle-right pattern/color symbolism zone, bottom wearing-sequence flowchart + summary band
Subject: must include a full-standing figure in authentic ethnic minority costume as central anchor; if object-focused theme, switch to central exploded-view diagram while preserving full infographic structure
Scene/backdrop: must be rice-white / silk-paper / pale tea-toned paper texture background, clean and archival
Lighting/mood: needs neutral, even, specimen-photography lighting — exact light quality is yours
Color palette: must be restrained and archival — muted earth tones, ink blacks, occasional mineral pigment accents; exact swatch selection is yours
Materials/textures: must include realistic fabric swatches (embroidery density, silver ornament patina, textile weave) with magnified detail callouts
Text (verbatim): must include Simplified Chinese throughout — top header with main title + subtitle + intro text, left zone with part labels on leader lines, upper-right zone with material captions, middle-right zone with pattern names and color symbolism, bottom zone with wearing sequence and core characteristics summary; all characters must be clear, regular, and readable
Constraints: must preserve authentic structural accuracy of ethnic minority costume systems, no invented patterns or anachronistic elements, all annotations must correspond to real components, text must be actual Chinese characters not gibberish or pinyin, overall tone must be scholarly and collection-worthy not commercial or decorative
Avoid: poster aesthetics, studio photography vibes, e-commerce styling, anime illustration, cosplay styling, random mislabeled parts, blurry text, fake materials, excessive ornamentation
